summary: DHH highlights Jim Weirich's new Story Cards application for planning Extreme Programming projects, built with Ruby on Rails. The app was developed in just over 1,000 lines of code and is already being used to plan real projects including Rake and Story Cards itself. DHH praises both the application's functionality and Jim's decision to share it as open source at such an early stage.
key_points: Jim Weirich built a functional XP planning tool called Story Cards using Ruby on Rails in just over 1KLOC
The application is already dogfooded — used to plan Rake, Story Cards itself, and a hospital calendar system
The source code is available on RubyForge as pre-alpha software for others to explore
money_quotes: He's been doing it all with Ruby on Rails and has in just over 1KLOC developed a very functional creation
Massive kudos to Jim for embarking on this cool application and sharing it with the world this early
key_insight: Rails enables rapid development of functional applications, as demonstrated by a full XP planning tool built in just over 1,000 lines of code.
